name: Multo
description: Multo, meaning 'ghost' or 'spirit' in Filipino, is a broad term encompassing a wide array of spectral beings in Filipino folklore. They are the souls of the dead, often lingering in the mortal realm due to unfinished business, tragic circumstances, or simply a desire to remain connected to the living. Multo can manifest in various forms, from vague shadows and disembodied voices to fully formed apparitions of deceased individuals. Their presence is often associated with feelings of unease, cold spots, and unexplained occurrences. Belief in multo is deeply ingrained in Filipino culture, influencing daily life and shaping rituals surrounding death and remembrance. Some multo are benevolent, acting as guardians or offering guidance, while others are malevolent, seeking vengeance or causing mischief. Respect and caution are generally advised when dealing with these entities, as their intentions can be unpredictable. The Multo serves as a constant reminder of the interconnectedness between the living and the dead in the Filipino worldview, and the importance of honoring ancestors and maintaining a respectful relationship with the spiritual realm. Different regions of the Philippines have their own specific names and variations of multo, each with unique characteristics and associated beliefs.
subtitle: Filipino ghosts and ancestral spirits
appearance: Multo can appear in various forms, often resembling the deceased person they once were. They can be translucent, shadowy, or even appear as solid figures. Some may exhibit signs of their death, such as wounds or injuries. Others might manifest as orbs of light or disembodied voices. The appearance of a multo often depends on its emotional state and the circumstances surrounding its death.
habitat: Multo can inhabit a wide range of locations, including old houses, cemeteries, forests, rivers, and places where they lived or died. They are often drawn to places with strong emotional ties or unresolved conflicts. Some multo are believed to be bound to specific objects or locations, while others are more mobile and can roam freely.
behavior: The behavior of multo varies greatly depending on their individual personalities and circumstances. Some are benevolent and act as guardians, protecting their loved ones or offering guidance. Others are malevolent and seek revenge for past wrongs or simply enjoy causing mischief. Many are simply lost and confused, wandering aimlessly and reliving their past lives. Respect and caution are generally advised when interacting with multo, as their intentions can be unpredictable.

keyMyths.title: The White Lady of Balete Drive
keyMyths.description: A popular urban legend tells of a woman in white who haunts Balete Drive in Quezon City, often appearing to drivers at night. She is believed to be a multo of a woman who died tragically in the area.
keyMyths.source: Various online sources and local testimonies

sources.author: Maximo Ramos
sources.work: The Creatures of Philippine Lower Mythology
sources.description: A comprehensive study of various creatures in Filipino folklore.
